name: Sage Botanical Bleed-Through Illustrated Landing
description: A single-column reading-flow landing page where full-bleed botanical line-art illustrations ARE the layout — large-scale ink drawings of fern fronds, eucalyptus branches, and root systems bleed across section boundaries and behind text blocks, with body copy wrapping around illustration shapes using CSS shape-outside, hand-lettered display numerals for statistics rendered in a brushy calligraphic weight, a muted sage-and-cream palette where every element is desaturated except a single deep forest green (#2D5A3D) pill-shaped CTA that appears exactly twice, and generous vertical rhythm where the botanical drawings create natural section breaks instead of horizontal rules — the page reads like a field guide that happens to sell something.

vibes.mood: Feels like a page torn from a Victorian botanical compendium that someone typeset wellness copy over — the fern illustrations were here first and the text arranged itself around them respectfully. The only color that demands anything of you is the forest-green CTA, which feels less like a button and more like a wax seal on a letter from an herbalist.
influences: ernst haeckel kunstformen der natur plates

meta.audience: Consumers who buy wellness products based on packaging and editorial taste — those who would frame a supplement label if the illustration was good enough and trust brands that look like they were designed by someone who has read Braiding Sweetgrass
